name: Go File-read Parity Plan Gate
decision: Plan the remaining Go read-only parity gap for text attachment body search, but do not implement file scanning in 23.1.

current_runtime_truth.python_behavior: Python GET /api/notes?q=... tokenizes the keyword, reads text attachment file bodies for md/markdown/txt attachments, and merges matching note_id values into the existing SQL search.
current_runtime_truth.go_behavior_before_23_1: Go GET /api/notes?q=... searches note fields, tags, and Note_Attachments title/file_path metadata only. It does not read attachment file bodies.
current_runtime_truth.go_live_route_truth: The hardened Go read-only route remains limited to GET /api/test, /api/categories, /api/tags, /api/notes, and numeric /api/notes/{id}.
python_owned_surfaces: Text attachment body search until 23.2 is explicitly authorized and verified
All POST / PUT / DELETE / PATCH routes
Files, uploads, cleanup, import/export, system/server, migrations, frontend/static assets, and unreviewed routes

file_read_contract.data_dir_rule: Go may read only under the explicit runtime --data-dir root. For local parity tests this must be a temporary copied data dir; for any later Pi candidate it must be the existing Prism data dir approved by a separate live gate.
file_read_contract.attachment_path_source: Use Note_Attachments.file_path as a relative path only. Treat empty, absolute, volume-qualified, UNC, URL-like, or malformed paths as non-matches.
path_canonicalization: Join data dir root and Note_Attachments.file_path.
Clean and resolve the candidate path before opening.
Reject any path containing parent traversal segments before or after normalization.
Reject candidates whose resolved path is not strictly inside the resolved data dir.
Reject symlinks or any resolved path that escapes the data dir.
Reject absolute external paths even if they point to an allowed extension.
file_read_contract.oversized_file_behavior: Skip the file as a non-match and do not fail the notes query.
file_read_contract.missing_file_behavior: Skip the file as a non-match and do not fail the notes query.
file_read_contract.unsupported_extension_behavior: Skip the file as a non-match and do not fail the notes query.
file_read_contract.encoding_policy: Read as UTF-8 text, accepting UTF-8 BOM and replacing invalid bytes; do not add charset detection or external dependencies.

timeout_performance_boundary.scope: Per request bounded read-only scan over candidate text attachments that survived SQL file_type filtering and path validation.
timeout_performance_boundary.on_limit_or_timeout: Stop scanning additional files and return the DB-backed result plus matches found before the limit. Do not fail the request.

side_effects_allowed: Bounded filesystem reads inside data dir
side_effects_forbidden: File writes, deletes, renames, chmod/chown, directory creation, uploads, extraction, cleanup, indexing cache writes, DB writes, migrations, service reloads, or network access

parity_fixture_plan.test_scope: Python vs Go diff using the same copied DB and controlled temporary attachment files under a copied data dir.

fixture_cases.expectation: A query matching Note_Attachments.title or file_path returns the same Python and Go note list without requiring body scan.
A query matching only an allowed .md attachment body returns the same Python and Go note list after 23.2 implementation.
A query matching only an allowed .markdown attachment body returns the same Python and Go note list after 23.2 implementation.
A query matching only an allowed .txt attachment body returns the same Python and Go note list after 23.2 implementation.
A missing attachment file is skipped as a non-match and the query still succeeds.
A file larger than 1048576 bytes is skipped as a non-match and the query still succeeds.
A file with an unsupported extension is skipped as a non-match even if its body contains the keyword.
A file_path containing parent traversal or resolving outside data dir is rejected before open and cannot produce a match.
An absolute or volume-qualified path is rejected before open and cannot produce a match.
Invalid UTF-8 bytes do not crash the request; replacement decoding still allows ordinary ASCII/UTF-8 tokens around invalid bytes to match.

required_23_2_tests: A source-level Go test or pytest lock for path canonicalization and traversal rejection.
A Python vs Go diff fixture extending tests/test_phase18_go_shadow_contract.py with controlled attachment files.
A negative test proving POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH and attachment write routes are still absent from Go.
A contract test proving Caddy/service/frontend defaults were not changed by file-read implementation.

runtime_boundary.rollback_owner: Python remains the owner for text attachment body search until Go file-read parity passes local copied-DB fixtures and a later live gate explicitly promotes it.
rollback_plan: Do not route Go file-read parity to production in 23.1.
If 23.2 local implementation fails safety or parity tests, remove the Go scanner changes and keep text attachment body search Python-owned.
If a later live candidate fails, remove the route/service change and return to Python-owned attachment body search using the existing hardened read-only surface boundaries.
stop_conditions: Any path traversal or symlink escape can be opened.
Any unsupported, oversized, missing, absolute, or external path causes request failure instead of a skipped non-match.
Go writes DB or filesystem state.
Go handles write methods or attachment write/file routes.
Caddy, systemd, frontend default API target, production DB, or public exposure changes appear without a separate authorization gate.

not_authorized_by_23_1: Go attachment file body scanner implementation
Changing prism-go-readonly.service away from SQLite query_only
Production knowledge.db access or writes from Go
Pi deploy or live service reload
New AI/ML dependency, charset detection dependency, indexing cache, or server-side search cache
verification_required_for_23_1: tests/test_phase23_go_file_read_parity_plan.py locks the file-read contract, fixture plan, runtime boundary, and 23.2 next-step limits
tests/test_phase20_go_read_surface_polish.py continues to document the pre-23.2 Python-owned body-search gap

result.conclusion: 23.1 authorizes only a plan and contract for Go text attachment body search parity. It does not implement scanning or change runtime routing.

allowed_next_step.title: Go File-read Parity Implementation Gate
allowed_next_step.scope: Implement a bounded read-only Go text attachment body scanner only inside the 23.1 data-dir/path/extension/size/performance contract, using local copied DB and controlled files first. This still must not change live Caddy, systemd, frontend defaults, production DB, writes/files/migrations, Python removal, or public exposure.
not_authorized_without_approval: Go attachment file body scanner implementation
Production knowledge.db access or writes from Go
Pi deploy or live service reload
